description: Seed research work scopes for hallmarks-of-aging evidence synthesis. Tracks bound agent work; they are not automatically meta-analysis strata.
design_notes: A track is a research work scope used to plan bounded search, screening, extraction, and coverage-repair passes.
Formal synthesis groups must be derived from intervention, population, comparator, endpoint, duration, design, and bias fields rather than from track ID alone.
Each track must include explicit inclusion, exclusion, and boundary notes so overlap with neighboring tracks is auditable.

source_basis.title: Hallmarks of aging: An expanding universe
source_basis.citation: Lopez-Otin C, Blasco MA, Partridge L, Serrano M, Kroemer G. Cell. 2023;186(2):243-278.

tracks.name: Senolytics
tracks.definition: Interventions intended to selectively eliminate senescent cells or senescent-cell subpopulations through direct cytotoxic or apoptosis-sensitizing mechanisms.
inclusion_criteria: Studies of compounds, biologics, or cell-directed approaches presented as directly clearing senescent cells.
Sources measuring senescent-cell burden, senescence markers, tissue function, healthspan, lifespan, clinical, or safety outcomes after a direct senolytic intervention.
Registry records for human trials testing direct senolytic interventions in aging-relevant or age-related disease contexts.
exclusion_criteria: SASP suppression or inflammatory modulation without intended senescent-cell clearance belongs in senomorphics or inflammatory tracks.
Immune-engineered clearance strategies belong in immune clearance of senescent cells unless the source explicitly evaluates a direct senolytic agent.
Oncology-only cytotoxic use is excluded unless the source makes an aging-relevant senescent-cell clearance claim.
boundary_notes: This is a work scope, not a pooling unit; D+Q, fisetin, BCL-family inhibitors, FOXO4-related approaches, and other branches require separate synthesis grouping before meta-analysis.
Findings may map to chronic inflammation or intercellular communication when senescent-cell clearance changes SASP or inflammatory signaling.
